creator: demelier.david@gmail.com
text:
On 04.09.2013 08:51, Ganael LAPLANCHE wrote:
> Hi David,
> 
> Thanks for your report.
> 
> The configure script looks for libumem and links to it if it is found,
> but it is *not* a requirement to build pcsxr (I presume libumem is not
> found when you run pcsxr because your had it installed when you built
> pcsxr and you then removed the umem port, right ?).
> 
> I may be wrong but I could not find any use of the library within
> pcsxr's source code, so I think the configure check for libumem is
> useless ; I'll have a look at that and remove it if it is the case.
> 
> Best regards,
> 
> --
> Ganael LAPLANCHE <ganael.laplanche@martymac.org>
> http://www.martymac.org | http://contribs.martymac.org
> FreeBSD: martymac <martymac@FreeBSD.org>, http://www.FreeBSD.org
> 

Hi,

Yes, in the reality I did have "WITHOUT_CDDL" in /etc/src.conf which
remove libumem from base system. And because pcsxr was built on a
GENERIC jail without any src.conf it didn't run on my final system.

And yes, it builds fine on my machine which don't have any libumem so
why does it links to? Maybe a configure failure.

Cheers,
